# References for Algorithmic Mechanism Models

## Core sources used for this algorithmic layer

- Covington, Adams & Sargin, *Deep Neural Networks for YouTube Recommendations* (Google Research / ACM RecSys 2016): https://research.google/pubs/pub45530/
- TikTok Support, *How TikTok recommends content*: https://support.tiktok.com/en/using-tiktok/exploring-videos/how-tiktok-recommends-content
- TikTok Newsroom, *How TikTok recommends videos #ForYou*: https://newsroom.tiktok.com/en-gb/how-tiktok-recommends-videos-foryou
- Chaney, Stewart & Engelhardt, *How algorithmic confounding in recommendation systems increases homogeneity and decreases utility*: https://bstewart.scholar.princeton.edu/publications/how-algorithmic-confounding-recommendation-systems-increases-homogeneity-and
- Klimashevskaia et al., *A survey on popularity bias in recommender systems*: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11257-024-09406-0
- Zoralioglu & Yalcin, *Dynamic feedback loops in recommender systems* (2026): https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10844-026-01025-y
- Yu et al./Springer, *A comprehensive survey on multi-behavior recommender systems* (2026): https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s41060-025-01019-z
- Muchnik, Aral & Taylor, *Social influence bias: a randomized experiment*: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23929980/
- Salganik, Dodds & Watts, *Experimental study of inequality and unpredictability in an artificial cultural market*: https://collaborate.princeton.edu/en/publications/experimental-study-of-inequality-and-unpredictability-in-an-artif
- Granovetter, *Threshold Models of Collective Behavior*: https://sociology.stanford.edu/publications/threshold-models-collective-behavior
- Watts, *A simple model of global cascades on random networks*: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC122850/
- Rogers & Righetti, *Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention*: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784
- Graham, Hames & Alpert, *The Coordination Network Toolkit*: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s42001-024-00260-z
- Luceri et al., *Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior on TikTok* (2025): https://www.isi.edu/results/publications/20550/coordinated-inauthentic-behavior-on-tiktok-challenges-and-opportunities-for-detection-in-a-video-first-ecosystem
- Yang & Niu, *A genre trust model for defending shilling attacks in recommender systems*: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40747-021-00357-2
- Choi et al., *Shilling Attacks Detection in Recommender Systems Based on Target Item Analysis*: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4519300/
